content:
* Why it wins: It prevents the vacancy loss that occurs when a town refuses to issue a CO due to missing lead paperwork.
C. The "Clean Comp" Pricing Engine (Antitrust Safe)
* The Problem: The report notes NY bans on "algorithmic price-fixing." Landlords are afraid to use tools like RealPage but still need data to set rents.
* The MVP Solution: A pricing tool that markets itself on data hygiene.
* Data Source: Explicitly scrapes only public listing data (StreetEasy, Zillow, Craigslist) and ignores private rent rolls.
* Feature: "Audit Trail" reporting—showing exactly which 5-10 public comparable listings were used to generate the price recommendation, proving no private competitor data was used.
* Why it wins: It gives landlords defensible pricing data that won't get them sued by the Attorney General.
Strategic Recommendation
Do not build a general CRM. The market is saturated (KVCore, Salesforce). Instead, build a "Compliance Widget" that integrates into those CRMs. Market it as a plugin: "The plugin that keeps your NY/NJ brokerage legal." This lowers the barrier to adoption and solves the specific pain points identified in your report.
